lab_signals.context: Elevated ferritin without clear co-occurring disease signals — could reflect iron overload, recent illness, or subclinical inflammation. Worth checking alongside serum iron and transferrin saturation.

Elevated ferritin without clear co-occurring disease signals — could reflect iron overload, recent illness, or subclinical inflammation. Worth checking alongside serum iron and transferrin saturation.

Elevated ferritin here likely reflects chronic inflammation or an ongoing condition (e.g. kidney, liver, or metabolic disease) rather than iron overload — note the co-occurring signals: LBXGH_glycohemoglobin, alt_u_l, ast_u_l, fasting_glucose_mg_dl, ggt_u_l.
